definition: The opacity of private credit markets systematically biases observable risk indicators toward false optimism. The direction of this bias is certain but the magnitude is uncertain, creating underappreciated systemic risk.
evidence: Structural analysis of private credit reporting incentives favoring optimism
Comparison of observable vs. estimated true underlying credit quality
Mark-to-model practices reducing reported volatility below economic reality
open_questions: What is the true magnitude of the optimism bias?
At what point does opacity itself become a trigger as credibility erodes?
How correlated are private credit exposures across ostensibly diversified portfolios?
